message:
fix(mailbox-verdict): decisive corruption beats recovery-lock precedence in compute_state_from_probes

`compute_state_from_probes` previously returned `MailboxState::Recovering`
whenever `recovery_lock_held` was true — short-circuiting before any
severity comparison, except for the explicit `MailboxState::Escalate`
exit. That shape meant a decisive `Broken` verdict from a sanity probe
(e.g. "primary SQLite read-path health probe failed") was masked as
`Recovering` if a recovery lock happened to be held at probe time, which
understated the true health state for operators.

Collapse the two exits into a single severity-aware check:

    if recovery_lock_held && state.severity() < MailboxState::Broken.severity() {
        MailboxState::Recovering
    } else {
        state
    }

The recovery-lock override only applies when the aggregate probe state is
strictly less severe than `Broken`. `Escalate` still wins via the
monotonic `state.max_severity(...)` accumulation above. `Broken` now
survives intact even under a recovery lock, which is the correct signal
for downstream alerts.

Regression test `decisive_corruption_beats_recovery_lock_precedence`
feeds a single `db_sanity` error probe with `recovery_lock_held=true` and
asserts `verdict.state == MailboxState::Broken`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
